task.title: Extend rebooking so passengers with connecting itineraries can be rerouted across multiple segments when a single leg is disrupted, while preserving business rules (priority ordering, seat availability, and “no seat” handling).dyfghjkl;
task.description:
h2. Background

Phase 1 rebooks onto the *next available direct flight* on the same origin/destination within a search window. Real networks often require *multi-hop* recovery (e.g., SFO→DEN→JFK) when nonstop capacity is gone.

h2. Acceptance criteria

# *Model / data*
#* Introduce an explicit representation of a *passenger itinerary* (single- or multi-segment) without breaking existing {{Booking}} rows (migration strategy documented in PR).
#* A booking references either a *single segment* (Phase 1 compatible) or belongs to an itinerary group.
# *Search*
#* Given a disrupted segment, the rebooking engine can propose *valid multi-hop paths* constrained by:
#** Minimum connection time between legs (configurable via env, e.g. {{MIN_CONNECTION_MINUTES}}).
#** Maximum additional travel time vs original itinerary (configurable).
#** Airport compatibility (same metro exceptions configurable; default strict).
# *Algorithm*
#* VIP > BUSINESS > ECONOMY ordering is preserved *within* each disruption batch.
#* If *no* valid path exists, passenger booking ends in {{NO_SEAT_AVAILABLE}} (or itinerary-level equivalent) and downstream compensation/notifications still run deterministically.
# *Correctness*
#* Unit tests cover at least:
#** 2-hop successful rebook
#** Impossible connection → {{NO_SEAT_AVAILABLE}}
#** Priority ordering with mixed loyalty on the same disrupted flight
# *Operational*
#* Structured logs include {{itineraryId}}, {{path}}, and {{correlationId}} for each rebook decision.

h2. Non-goals (for this ticket)

* Global optimization across the entire network (that is a separate research spike).

h2. Definition of done

* Migrations applied, tests green, README section updated for new env vars and behavior.

task.goal: Implement the requested feature end-to-end with tests.
constraints: Respect existing project structure and coding conventions.
Add or update tests for new behavior or bug fixes.
Implement behavior exactly as requested in the ticket.
